title: More Slabs And Stairs
description: Adds slab and stair variants of coal, iron, gold, diamond, lapis lazuli, emerald, netherite, calcite, quartz brick, obsidian, bedrock, end stone, concrete, and terracotta blocks.
body:
This Minecraft Fabric mod adds slab and stair variants for coal, iron, diamond, emerald, gold, lapis lazuli, obsidian, bedrock, calcite, quartz bricks, end stone, and all colors of concrete and terracotta. All slab and stair blocks have the same hardness, blast resistance, and required tool as their base blocks.

Slabs and stairs from the mod can be crafted from their base blocks using the crafting table or the stonecutter, except for bedrock slabs and stairs, which can only be crafted using the crafting table. Since bedrock slabs and stairs must be crafted using bedrock blocks, they are still unobtainable in survival without cheats.

Coal slabs and stairs can be used as furnace fuel - each coal slab smelts 40 items while each coal stair smelts 60 items.

The mod also allows quartz blocks to be reverse-crafted into 4 nether quartz, similar to other mineral blocks.

Both vanilla slabs and slabs from this mod can be re-crafted into their base blocks as follows:![Four slabs arranged in a square in a crafting table produces two full blocks.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c9dc7b32529aeee20bd0b7ef9ce318afb5d7789f.png)

The mod is available in the following languages. If you see any errors in my translations, go to the [Issues page on Github](https://github.com/Crossover661/More-Slabs-and-Stairs/issues) and create an issue.
- English (US, UK, Canada, Australia, New Zealand)
- Spanish (Argentina, Chile, Ecuador, Spain, Mexico, Uruguay, Venezuela)
- Portuguese (Brazil, Portugal)
- French (France, Canada)
- German (Germany, Austria)
- Italian (Italy)
